items.mistake: Imputing with mean or median computed on the full dataset before train/test split
items.fix: Fit SimpleImputer, KNNImputer, or group medians on X_train only; call transform on validation and test with those learned statistics.
items.why: Holdout rows influence fill values, leaking future information and optimistic evaluation scores.
items.example: If test contains only high-income applicants, a global income median imputer trained on train+test lets the model indirectly see test demographics during preprocessing.

items.mistake: Treating sentinel codes like -1 or 999 as real measurements
items.fix: Map domain-specific placeholders to NaN during audit, then apply explicit imputation or a dedicated "unknown" category.
items.why: Sentinels look numeric but encode missingness; models interpret them as extreme valid values.
items.example: A hospital uses `999` for "weight not taken"; leaving it as 999 makes a linear model think the patient weighs 999 kg.

items.mistake: Scaling numeric features before imputing NaN values
items.fix: Impute first (or use models that accept NaN), then fit scalers on the imputed training matrix.
items.why: Standardizers cannot compute mean and variance on NaN; reordering steps avoids errors and inconsistent pipelines.
items.example: StandardScaler on raw columns with 30% NaN either fails or forces a separate fill step that should have been explicit upstream.

items.mistake: Using listwise deletion when half the rows are incomplete
items.fix: Impute, add indicators, or use learners with native missing support; reserve deletion for low missing rates with MCAR justification.
items.why: Dropping most rows amplifies variance and can discard minority classes entirely.
items.example: Deleting any row with a missing optional survey field removes 60% of a churn dataset and erases most recent sign-ups.
